questions.question: How does the bit-flip code encode the logical state |ψ⟩ = α|0⟩ + β|1⟩?
questions.explanation: The bit-flip code maps |0⟩ → |000⟩ and |1⟩ → |111⟩. A general state α|0⟩ + β|1⟩ becomes α|000⟩ + β|111⟩, an entangled 3-qubit state (not a tensor product of three copies).

questions.question: What are the stabilizer generators for the bit-flip code?
questions.explanation: The stabilizers Z₁Z₂ and Z₂Z₃ compare parities between adjacent qubits. Both |000⟩ and |111⟩ are +1 eigenstates of these operators. Measuring them reveals X errors without disturbing the superposition.

questions.question: If the syndrome measurement gives (s₁=1, s₂=1), which correction should be applied?
questions.explanation: Syndrome (1,1) means qubit 2 disagrees with both qubit 1 (s₁=1) and qubit 3 (s₂=1). This identifies qubit 2 as the error location. Apply X₂ to correct it.

questions.question: Why does the bit-flip code fail to protect against Z (phase) errors?

options.text: Z errors are too rare to matter
Z errors commute with the stabilizers, so syndrome is always (0,0)
Z errors can only be corrected by measuring in the X basis
The code needs more qubits to detect Z errors

questions.explanation: Z errors commute with the Z₁Z₂ and Z₂Z₃ stabilizers, so they don't change the syndrome. A Z error on any qubit maps |+⟩_L to |−⟩_L (a logical error) while the syndrome remains (0,0).

questions.question: How many X errors can the bit-flip code correct?
questions.explanation: The code has distance d=3 for X errors, so it can correct t=⌊(d-1)/2⌋=1 X error. With 2 X errors, the syndrome would point to the wrong qubit, and 'correction' would cause a third error, creating a logical X error.

questions.question: How does the encoding circuit create entanglement without cloning?

options.text: CNOT gates create entanglement between data and ancilla qubits
The state is copied then entangled

questions.explanation: CNOT gates entangle the original qubit with ancillas initialized to |0⟩. For |0⟩, nothing happens (stays |000⟩). For |1⟩, both ancillas flip (becomes |111⟩). A superposition α|0⟩+β|1⟩ becomes the entangled state α|000⟩+β|111⟩.

questions.question: Syndrome measurement reveals whether the encoded data is |0⟩_L or |1⟩_L.
questions.explanation: False. Syndrome measurement only reveals error information. Both |000⟩ and |111⟩ give syndrome (0,0), and both X₁|000⟩ and X₁|111⟩ give syndrome (1,0). The syndrome is the same regardless of which logical state is encoded.
